mnemonics: CRISP-DM for Test: Cross-Validate (C), Report (R), Interpret (I), Split (S), Predict (P) - Diagnose Model (DM)
keyPoints: The core purpose of a test set is to provide an unbiased evaluation of a final model's performance on unseen data.
Proper data splitting (e.g., 70% train / 30% test) is crucial to avoid data leakage and ensure the test set remains a pure holdout sample.
Key metrics for evaluation depend on the model type (e.g., RMSE for regression, Confusion Matrix for classification).
commonMistakes: Testing on the training data, which gives a deceptively optimistic performance estimate (overfitting).
Preprocessing (e.g., scaling, centering) the entire dataset before splitting, which causes data leakage from the test set into the training process.
